races.name: Every Raceday Is Autism Friendly At Pontefract Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Showtown
runners.spotlight: Gelded after debut then posted consistent efforts over 6f at Pontefract (once) and Hamilton (twice); solid contender and again has place claims, assuming he returns from layoff in similar form.
runners.comment: Solid contender, assuming his consistency is maintained on return from layoff

runners.name: Invincible Boy
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f last autumn, finishing 1l second on good going at York then only 6l third (beaten favourite) on heavy at Doncaster; leading player on the debut form and looks open to progress back on forecast faster ground.
runners.comment: Leading player on debut form (second at York) and open to progress back on faster ground

runners.name: Agric Ranger
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal; by Aclaim; dam unraced sibling to two 5f winners, out of 6f Group 3 winning half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Profitable; one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: 10,000gns foal; by Aclaim; one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive

runners.name: Spaceman
runners.spotlight: Starman colt who showed clear promise with joint-second of 15, running on, at Redcar (6f, good) five months ago; appears likely to win races this year and commands respect.
runners.comment: Showed clear promise with joint-second of 15 at Redcar; appears likely to win races

runners.name: Tip Foster
runners.spotlight: Debutant by Dream Ahead and first foal of unraced half-sister to winners Dream Mover (5.5f-7f) and Taper Tantrum (7f/1m 2yo and useful 2m hurdle/chase); yard also runs King's Road Dandy; market helpful.
runners.comment: Debutant by Dream Ahead; yard also runs King's Road Dandy; market helpful

runners.name: Davenport Lass
runners.spotlight: Â£3,000 yearling; by Coulsty; sibling to four winners, notably Washaar (7f/1m 2yo including Listed; RPR 104); stablemate of Milford Grange; market informative.
runners.comment: Â£3,000 yearling; by Coulsty; stablemate of Milford Grange; market informative

runners.name: Star With Purpose
runners.spotlight: 19,000gns yearling; by Zoustar; dam Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 100), half-sister to useful 5f-6f winner Roulston Scar; one of the newcomers; check the betting.
runners.comment: 19,000gns yearling; by Zoustar; one of the newcomers; check the betting

runners.name: Milford Grange
runners.spotlight: Rallying third of eight, faring best of the newcomers, at Redcar (6f, good) in sole 2yo start; similar type to King's Road Dandy and improvement looks possible.
runners.comment: Rallying third at Redcar; similar type to King's Road Dandy; improvement possible

runners.name: Eva The Deeva
runners.spotlight: Recorded a meagre RPR in finishing sixth of 11 at Newcastle (7f, AW) where she was friendless in the betting.
runners.comment: Recorded a meagre RPR in 7f AW contest

runners.name: Go Teejay
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a couple of 6f events (turf/AW); likely to be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Likely to be more interesting in handicaps shortly

runners.name: Rousham
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard, despite wearing a hood, and was always in rear in Newcastle maiden (7f, AW); needs to derive major benefit from the drop in trip.
runners.comment: Always in rear in 7f AW event

runners.name: Kings Road Dandy
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing third of eight in C&D maiden (good to firm) eight months ago on debut; ran to an ordinary RPR but looks open to improvement; heed the market signals for further guidance.
runners.comment: Absent and gelded since finishing third over C&D eight months ago; open to progress

races.name: Boyzlife Live At Ponte Friday 22nd May Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Arabian Desert
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who showed progressive form in defeat last autumn, culminating in good second behind the market leader in the Horris Hill at Newbury (7f, soft); went close over 1m at York (good) the time before; clear top on the figures.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Horris Hill at Newbury on final autumn start; clear top on the figures

runners.name: Regulus Black
runners.spotlight: Modest RPRs in a couple of AW events but may well improve for this switch to grass, being closely related to two turf winners for his connections; interesting in that respect.
runners.comment: Interesting switched to grass; closely related to turf winners for his connections

runners.name: Seattle Chief
runners.spotlight: Just ordinary RPRs in a couple of Southwell AW contests but this change of surface may prompt a bigger effort, making him a similar type to Regulus Black.
runners.comment: May improve for the switch to turf; similar type to Regulus Black

runners.name: Clough
runners.spotlight: Son of Mastercraftsman and first foal of 1m4f winner (RPR 90); has had gelding/wind operations; sole newcomer in the field and it's his belated debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: Son of Mastercraftsman; makes belated debut; market instructive
runners.owner: Matt Broughton Chris Hughes & D Cunha

runners.name: Haayimm
runners.spotlight: Gelded since showing promise with third-place finish at Beverley (8.5f, soft) where he was the sole newcomer in a field of eight; open to progress and is one of the better contenders.
runners.comment: Gelded since showing promise with third of eight at Beverley; open to progress

runners.name: Contemplation
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find on his form for James Ferguson, reflected by the fact he'd be getting 48lb from Arabian Desert if this were a handicap; sold for 6,000gns since last run.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find on his form for James Ferguson

runners.name: August Spring
runners.spotlight: Holds very weak claims on his form shown in a soft-ground bumper and two AW novice events.
runners.comment: Very weak claims on form

runners.name: Pop The Question
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdles winner; modest fourth of six at Newcastle (7f, AW) on the belated switch to this code; others preferred.
runners.comment: Point/hurdles winner; posted a modest effort on Flat debut

races.name: Racing TV Sky Channel 424 Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Alpha Capture
runners.spotlight: Listed winner for William Haggas and handicap scorer for Willie Mullins; close third at Newcastle (1m, AW) on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark back on turf.
runners.comment: Close third at Newcastle on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark

runners.name: Kokanee
runners.spotlight: Record is 1-10, the success off current mark at Beverley (1m2f, good) on reappearance last year; ran creditably over C&D (soft) in October; respected on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Gained sole win on reappearance last year; on the same mark; respected on seasonal debut

runners.name: Poets Dawn
runners.spotlight: Veteran who showed he retains plenty of vitality by winning twice last season; on a competitive mark but his reappearance record doesn't augur particularly well.
runners.comment: Veteran; on a workable mark but his reappearance record isn't fully convincing

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Presents a dilemma as he's capable of being involved on AW form, beaten only 2l in higher grade most recently, but hasn't raced on turf since August 2023 when 16lb lower.
runners.comment: Capable of being involved on AW form but hasn't raced on turf since 2023
runners.owner: S McMillan F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Uppercase
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; best effort when 1l second off current mark over C&D (solid form; sole 1m attempt) last July; interesting returned to this scenario on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; best effort when second over C&D (solid form) last July; interesting

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler since last Flat attempt in 2024; gained sole success on AW early that year; no guarantees that he's worth current mark returned to this code.
runners.comment: No guarantees that he's worth current mark returned to the Flat

runners.name: Zowal
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his overall form since handicapping but ran encouragingly (sixth of 22) in the Spring Mile at Doncaster on reappearance and this is a drop in class; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly in the Spring Mile on reappearance; drops in class; hard to dismiss

runners.name: Not Me
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden whose AW form has dipped in two starts for new yard, latest in November; returns to turf for first time since 2023; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Maiden; form has dipped in two starts for new yard, latest in November

runners.name: Typical Woman
runners.spotlight: Absent since prevailing at Lingfield (1m1f, good to firm) eight months ago; no certainty to follow up, having raced mainly beyond today's trip and never won when fresh.
runners.comment: Absent since prevailing at Lingfield eight months ago; has never scored when fresh

races.name: Racing TV Sky Channel 424 Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Salaria
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-7 for current yard, the wins at Pontefract (1m2f, good to soft) and on AW, including at Lingfield (1m) on seasonal debut; this improving filly warrants respect.
runners.comment: Record of 4-7 for current yard, the wins at Pontefract and on AW; improving filly

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Has a modest strike-rate since his French days but is attractively weighted off same mark as for sole British win, which was gained on AW in December; inconsistent subsequently.
runners.comment: Attractively weighted off same mark as for sole British win; inconsistent since

runners.name: Mount King
runners.spotlight: Has registered three of his four wins over C&D, the most recent off just 1lb lower last June; ran respectably at Newmarket when last seen; one for the shortlist back here.
runners.comment: Has registered three of his four wins over C&D, the most recent last June; in the mix

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: Has spent most of his career as a sprinter; ran respectably over 7f on AW last time but doesn't leave the impression he's crying out for this further step up in distance.
runners.comment: Doesn't leave the impression he's crying out for this further step up in distance

runners.name: Pink Azalea
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps until form dipped sharply in last two appearances; chance of a revival weakens if the ground isn't soft (gained all three wins on that surface).
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps until form dipped sharply on last two starts

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Successful five times (1m-1m2f) on AW since December and ran well at Southwell (1m3f) last week; likely player, provided the form is transferred back to turf; 1-14 in this sphere.
runners.comment: Five wins on AW since December; ran well at Southwell last week; returns to turf

runners.name: Distinction
runners.spotlight: Successful the last two times he raced at Pontefract (1m, soft; 1m2f, good) and could go well off a workable mark back here with Doncaster reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Successful the last two times he raced at Pontefract; could go well back here

runners.name: Sailthisshipalone
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-21 for Richard Fahey; successful in most recent turf attempt but the market may prove best guide on stable debut; tongue-tie added; still in same ownership.
runners.comment: Record of 2-21 for Richard Fahey; market may prove best guide on stable debut

runners.name: Swift Salian
runners.spotlight: Seasonal debutant who could be ready to roll, having commenced last term with form figures of 4211; close second off current mark on penultimate outing; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Seasonal debutant who could be ready to roll; started last term with form figures of 4211

races.name: Ponte Premiership New For 2026 Handicap

runners.name: Arklow Lad
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was a turf scorer in Ireland; couple of good performances on AW for shrewd new connections, justifying favouritism at Chelmsford (5f) then third at Wolverhampton (6f; winner franked the form on Good Friday); remains open to further progress, provided he takes to added cheekpieces; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Couple of good performances on AW for shrewd new connections; open to further progress

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a success and creditable effort over 5f at Newcastle (AW); remains unexposed at this distance; sole attempt at Pontefract resulted in a reappearance win (6f, good) last April; respected back here on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over 5f; sole Pontefract attempt resulted in reappearance win last April

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: Return to 6f backfired last time when bidding to take his AW record to 3-3; only 2-18 on turf but enters calculations in this assignment, having gained one of the wins off 3lb higher over C&D (good).
runners.comment: Enters calculations, having gained one of his two turf wins off 3lb higher over C&D

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: Beaten only 2l, despite traffic issues, in both AW runs this year; record of 4-6 over C&D features two wins in the month of April; 4lb out of the weights back up in grade but Tom Kiely-Marshall takes off a handy 5lb; clear possibilities back here.
runners.comment: Record of 4-6 over C&D features two wins in the month of April; clear possibilities

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Listed winner for his previous yards; ran respectably in 5f AW handicap at Southwell on stable/seasonal debut; capable of being involved, albeit has an inferior strike-rate on turf.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in AW event on stable/seasonal debut; has an inferior strike-rate on turf
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Elegant Erin
runners.spotlight: In great form on soft ground last autumn but was punished by the assessor for a Listed effort that possibly flatters her; never figured in AW event two months ago; current mark seems difficult.
runners.comment: In great form on soft ground last autumn but current mark seems difficult

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Respectable second over C&D in most recent turf attempt and on AW when last seen, both Class 4; no certainty to go one better, having failed to score in this higher grade since 2022.
runners.comment: Respectable second over C&D in most recent turf attempt; this is a better race

runners.name: Spartan Arrow
runners.spotlight: Not solid on form shown in UAE and Britain this year but is very useful on his day, being a turf Listed and AW Group 3 scorer last term, as well as close second in the Epsom "Dash".
runners.comment: Not solid on 2026 form but is very useful on his day; Listed/Group winner last term
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXI And Partner

runners.name: Wobwobwob
runners.spotlight: One of the oldest contenders in this field and has done his winning over 6f/7f, mostly on ground softer than good; could well be tapped for toe in this belated first attempt at the minimum trip.
runners.comment: One of the oldest contenders and could well be tapped for toe dropped to 5f

runners.name: Havana Rum
runners.spotlight: Successful on AW in most recent 5f attempt and is far less exposed at this trip than 6f; however, he's rather in the deep end at this level returned to turf; yet to win above Class 4.
runners.comment: Far less exposed over 5f than 6f but he's rather in the deep end at this level

runners.name: Atomic Force
runners.spotlight: Had a long spell in Hong Kong; gradually back in business since returning to Britain and gained 5f AW wins the last twice; still attractively treated, considering he was a dual Group scorer on turf as a 2yo.
runners.comment: Gained 5f AW wins the last twice; dual Group scorer on turf as a 2yo; in the mix

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Losing spell goes back to his Palace House success two years ago; inconsistent last term for new yard and was well behind new stablemate Purosangue in the Ayr Gold Cup on final outing.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last term for new yard; well behind new stablemate Purosangue on final start

runners.name: Purosangue
runners.spotlight: Capable of useful form for Andrew Balding; close fourth when favourite for last year's Ayr Gold Cup; hasn't won since 2023 but looks interesting off current mark in this field on debut for new yard; remains with same owner; drops back to 5f for first time since good second (joint-best effort) in Sandown Group 3 in July 2024.
runners.comment: Capable of useful form for Andrew Balding; interesting in this field on stable debut

races.name: Tommy Hepple Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Fleur De Mer
runners.spotlight: Placed over 1m6f on last three AW starts; returns to turf on a workable mark and should be fine back at 1m4f; big player if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect.
runners.comment: Placed over 1m6f on last three AW starts; back in trip with cheekpieces added here
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

runners.name: Lightening Company
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than when second here (1m2f, soft) last October but did not emerge from the rear division when a big-priced outsider for all three subsequent AW runs; considered only if much strong in betting today.
runners.comment: Second over C&D last October but well down the field all three subsequent AW starts
runners.owner: Middleham Park Cxv Lycett Rcg & Partner

runners.name: Starshine Legend
runners.spotlight: Scored in first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut for James Ferguson in June 2024 and signed off for him with good effort in first-time blinkers the following September; makes stable debut after 580-day absence and with no headgear but is versatile regarding ground; market helpful.
runners.comment: Makes stable debut after a long absence; market helpful

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Absent since below-par AW run (without usual cheekpieces) last September but ran well when fresh last season and subsequently won off today's mark over 1m2f; also suited by 1m4f; one to consider.
runners.comment: Absent since below-par run in September but ran well when fresh last season; a possible

runners.name: Cloudy Rose
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, the latest off today's mark at Doncaster (extended 1m6f, good); also suited by 1m4f but ended that campaign on a low note and her fitness must be taken on trust.
runners.comment: Won twice last season but ended that campaign on a low note

runners.name: Swinging London
runners.spotlight: Returned to the Flat with respectable fourth of six at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last month but is still 3lb higher than for narrow AW win last autumn, and his Flat turf strike-rate is 0-14.
runners.comment: Ran okay on AW when back on the Flat last month but turf Flat strike-rate is 0-14

runners.name: Ashdown Forest
runners.spotlight: Not seen on the Flat since well-held third on heavy ground for Ed Walker last autumn and subsequent hurdling form for Jedd O'Keeffe was not particularly inspiring; sports first-time cheekpieces; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Returns to the Flat after some uninspiring hurdle form; sports new cheekpieces

runners.name: Turn And Finish
runners.spotlight: Became rather disappointing over hurdles for Harry Derham last year and finished down the field when returning in 1m4f AW handicap for new stable last month; needs that recent outing to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Became disappointing over hurdles last year; down the field on AW for new yard last month

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who made quite pleasing seasonal debut when keeping-on fourth in Southwell novice (1m3f, AW) last month; up in trip for this handicap debut, and should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Made pleasing seasonal debut in AW novice; can improve again in handicaps

runners.name: Johnny Boom
runners.spotlight: Caused 28-1 surprise on soft ground at Leiester (1m4f) in the autumn but was below that form on his two AW starts in February (wore cheekpieces) and is too inconsistent to rely upon; no headgear today.
runners.comment: Surprise winner on soft ground in October but too inconsistent to rely upon

runners.name: Secret Alliance
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three AW maiden/novice races (1m3f-1m4f) last summer/autumn and needs to take a big step forward on this seasonal/turf/handicap debut
runners.comment: Well beaten in three AW maiden/novice races (1m3f-1m4f) last summer/autumn

runners.name: Very Muddy Waters
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; didn't run badly when sixth at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last November but has fitness to prove after five-month break and was well beaten over C&D on only previous 1m4f attempt.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden; ran okay on AW when last in action, but that was five months ago

runners.name: Suitcase Smith
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only four starts but was soundly beaten after racing too freely on handicap debut at Yarmouth (11.5f, good to soft) in October and this is his first run since.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on handicap debut last October and not seen since

races.name: OAP Free Day Tuesday 21st April Handicap

runners.name: The Tunguska Event
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his five handicap runs, more recently when beating Dream Forever at Newcastle eight weeks ago; better drawn than his old rival today and may confirm the placings despite 5lb worse terms.
runners.comment: Won 2 of his 5 AW h'cap runs; up 5lb for latest but could have more to come back on turf

runners.name: Liquid Cooled
runners.spotlight: Left the form of his first three runs well behind when powering home to win a 6f handicap at Newcastle four weeks ago; this is a deeper race on a different surface, and off a 5lb higher mark, but it would be no surprise to see another big run.
runners.comment: Big step forward when winning on handicap debut last month (6f, AW); contender up 5lb

runners.name: Trucial Pearl
runners.spotlight: Currently 0-8 but she has shown more than enough in AW handicaps this year to think races could fall her way; both turf runs on good to firm (encouragement for slower on breeding); wide draw to overcome.
runners.comment: Leaves the impression a bigger run is lurking but she has a wide stall to overcome

runners.name: Baldosa
runners.spotlight: Ran as well as she ever has when third of seven at Newcastle 17 days ago, her first run for 172 days; should be sharper with that behind her but this will be the slowest turf she will have encountered.
runners.comment: Satisfactory third on seasonal return (6f, AW); unraced on slower than good on turf

runners.name: Maynora
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; bits of form that offer hope and his latest effort, when too keen in a steadily run 7f event at Newcastle, isn't the run to judge him on; seemingly exposed but goes without headgear today and the stable won this race in 2024.
runners.comment: Seemingly exposed but bits of form that bring him into the picture; no headgear today

runners.name: Antique Blue
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience in one run for Andrew Balding last June (6f, AW; wore cheekpieces); two heavy defeats for Ruth Carr, the latest at Newcastle (7f) 19 days ago; hooded for his handicap debut; bred to be useful but he is not a solid option.
runners.comment: Hooded for handicap debut; bred to be useful but he is not a solid option
runners.owner: Miss K Padgett & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Telling Time
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; ran his best race so far when second of ten at Wolverhampton three weeks ago (6f); more on his plate in this field and he has a wide stall to contend with.
runners.comment: Latest AW 2nd represented improvement; wide stall to overcome back on turf

runners.name: Miss Boyd
runners.spotlight: Some promise on turf last August; struggled on AW since, including both handicap runs, but she has dropped 7lb and returns to turf at a realistic level; too soon to write her off.
runners.comment: Some promise on turf last summer; quiet on AW since; may do better back on the grass

runners.name: Mystical Land
runners.spotlight: Second to an unexposed rival at Newcastle in January (5f) off this mark; not so good in either run since but he was too free on the first occasion and then unsuited by a messy event last time; unraced on turf slower than good; still has a bigger run in him.
runners.comment: 2nd (5f, AW) off this mark in January; less good twice since but there have been excuses

runners.name: Dream Forever
runners.spotlight: Chased home The Tunguska Event at Newcastle (6f) in February and meets that rival on 5lb better terms for 1.25l; didn't run badly over 7f back at Newcastle last month when not ideally positioned; can win races off this mark but he will have to overcome stall 14 if this is to be one of them.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts on AW this year; can win races but perhaps not this one from stall 14

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to his improved showing in a 7f novice at Newcastle in December; well beaten in a 6f handicap back at Newcastle in January but he still has time on his side and now drops into a 0-60.
runners.comment: December's Newcastle fourth was promising; ran poorly on handicap debut; unexposed

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: No better than midfield in three maiden/novice runs on AW; more realistic task today; the betting should provide clues.
runners.comment: Modest form on AW this winter; more realistic task now handicapping; market useful

runners.name: Miss Magic Dragon
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and low-level form in three AW runs in late 2025; bred to do much better at some point and she needs a close market check from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Modest form in three AW runs in late 2025; could prove different proposition now h'capping

runners.name: Proud Mary
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three quick runs on turf last summer (5f-6f; good or faster ground); likely to do better at some point this year.
runners.comment: Didn't achieve a great deal as a 2yo but should find her level in minor handicaps

races.name: Sue Sheppard Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Get Up Everybody
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but clear signs he was getting the hang of racing last year, placed in three of his last four starts; he has had a wind operation since last seen; market useful back from a six-month absence.
runners.comment: 0-9 but placed in 3 of his last 4 starts in 2025; had wind op since latest; market useful

runners.name: Sergeant Mayer
runners.spotlight: Came to hand early last year and he returns from a 168-day break off a mark just 1lb higher than for his Ripon win 11 months ago; the absence of cheekpieces tempers enthusiasm this time.
runners.comment: In good form last spring; feasible mark now back from 168 days off; usual headgear absent

runners.name: Pinjarra
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who has won three 6f events on AW since August; not at his best at Southwell last month and he has a wide stall to overcome returned to 5f.
runners.comment: Prominent racer; three 6f AW wins since August; drops back to 5f from a wide stall

runners.name: Ganesha
runners.spotlight: Well short of his best on his last three starts; now returns to Class 6 company off a reduced mark but he has been done no favours by the draw.
runners.comment: Well short of best on last three starts; drops in class but has the widest stall to defy

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: In good form in the second half of 2025, winning three times over 5f; not on a bad mark for her return but she has a 161-day absence to defy.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in good form; fair mark if ready to roll after her 161-day absence

runners.name: Masterclass
runners.spotlight: Hold-up sprinter who is on the same mark as when seeing off nine rivals at Nottingham last May (5f, good to firm); in and out afterwards but the return of blinkers should help now back from 138 days off.
runners.comment: Hold-up sprinter; back to last winning mark and the return of blinkers should help

runners.name: Lucius Aurelius
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden but has plenty of ability for one of his rating; unable to show what he could do at Southwell four weeks ago when trapped behind a wall of horses from 2f out; double-figure draw to contend with today but he should be winning soon.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden but on a dangerous mark and latest run is easy to forgive; tricky draw
runners.owner: John Kelly John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt

runners.name: Kitaab
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on 2024 form; taken a while to find his feet this year after a spell on the sidelines but he retains sufficient ability to play a leading role; will need luck in running back at 5f but suspicion he's ready to run a big race.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win and while perhaps best at 6f, he can't be discounted; will need luck

runners.name: Harb
runners.spotlight: Successful at Newcastle in February (5f) off 2lb lower; solid effort next time and when down the field on his latest start it was over 6f; can't rule out back down in trip.
runners.comment: Not at best over 6f last time; earlier 5f efforts bring him right into the reckoning

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Twice made all at Beverley last summer (5f, good and good to firm); shaped as though the run was needed on his return at Wolverhampton 18 days ago; on a handy mark; considered.
runners.comment: Two Beverley wins last summer; dangerous mark if recent AW return has sharpened him up
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Mu Mu Land
runners.spotlight: 6f AW maiden winner as a 2yo; 0-7 in handicaps last year but he dropped 13lb in the weights as a result; had a wind op soon after latest run in December; drops to 5f for the first time since his debut; wide draw.
runners.comment: Dropped 13lb in a blank 3yo campaign; had wind op since latest; drops to 5f; tricky draw

runners.name: Wrestling Revenue
runners.spotlight: Finished third in the corresponding race last year (good ground) and won a C&D handicap in October off a 4lb lower mark; disappointed twice since but he has landed a good draw back at Pontefract and a revival would be no surprise.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year and then won over C&D in October; good draw; could revive

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, mainly at Hamilton, and she starts this campaign off a manageable mark and from the inside stall; first time out hasn't been the time to catch her and her apprentice is having her first ride under rules.
runners.comment: Prolific winner; on a handy mark but first time out hasn't been the time to catch her

runners.name: Monotone
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his form for Jim Bolger but he has finished down the field in four runs for new connections, weakening over 7f and 6f; the return of a tongue tie may help, as will the drop into a Class 6, so not discounted back at 5f for the owners who took this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Drops in trip and class with a tongue tie back on; a revival isn't out of the question

races.name: Happy Birthday Fiona Walker Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Klervia
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles for Barry Brennan; 25-1 and tailed off on stable debut over 2m7f here three weeks ago; that was after 256 days off but she has a lot to prove; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 25-1 and tailed off on stable debut over 2m7f here three weeks ago

runners.name: Locked Down Lad
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; 0-13 over hurdles; has placed form to his name and cheekpieces could be an interesting addition as he did some hanging at Chepstow last time.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces could be an interesting addition as he did some hanging at Chepstow last time

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: Twice third in five races since arriving from Ireland so can't be entirely dismissed despite a miserable run last time in first-time cheekpieces; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Placed before so not entirely dismissed despite a miserable run last time

runners.name: Blue In The West
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with two good efforts over 2m7f here last month; didn't jump remotely well enough to give himself a chance at Chepstow; back here and provided he organises himself at the hurdles, he's a likely contender.
runners.comment: Jumped poorly last time; previous placed efforts here over 2m7f offer hope

runners.name: Future Times
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden who only recently returned from a mammoth absence; beaten about 14l on both occasions and behind Vengeance last time.
runners.comment: Beaten about 14l in two runs after an absence; behind Vengeance last time

runners.name: Vengeance
runners.spotlight: No wins in 13 but ran okay two weeks ago at Wincanton (2m4f, good), finishing under 8l behind the winner who comfortably followed up last week at Ludlow; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Beaten under 8l at Wincanton and the winner comfortably followed up last week at Ludlow

runners.name: Pillar The Poet
runners.spotlight: Left the Newland/Insole stable for 1,000gns last July, since when he's finished third in a point and a remote fourth on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f; 16-1).
runners.comment: Third in a point before remote fourth on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f; 16-1)

runners.name: Dawns Desire
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and best effort yet when third of five runners in her third handicap start at Fontwell (2m5f, soft); if coping with this surface then clearly has some sort of chance; 1lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Improved third at Fontwell; if okay on this drier ground then should have some chance

runners.name: Edna E Mode
runners.spotlight: Interesting on some of last year's form on good ground but nothing in two handicaps (on soft and good) since returning from a long break.
runners.comment: Nothing in two handicaps (on soft and good) since returning from a long break

runners.name: Alderley Charlie
runners.spotlight: He'll be glad to see the back of fences after producing form figures of PFPP, not that a case can be made on hurdle exploits; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: He'll be glad to see the back of fences after producing form figures of PFPP

runners.name: Greedy Smith
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred but hasn't raised his modest profile in two handicaps at about 3m the last twice; cheekpieces need to do something.
runners.comment: Pulled up and tailed off in two handicaps; cheekpieces need to do something

runners.name: Noble Grace
runners.spotlight: Very disappointing over hurdles given she ran so well in a Cheltenham bumper on debut; has performed below market expectations in two handicaps but both were on soft ground so she may run better under these conditions with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Regressed since debut but now back on better ground with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Urban Isle
runners.spotlight: Only minor promise in bumpers and beaten a long way in all three runs over hurdles, albeit two of those races were won by the first two home in the Supreme at Cheltenham; entitled to be far more competitive in this company on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Pretty poor so far but entitled to be more competitive in this company on handicap debut

races.name: Travel Incentive For British Buyers Novices' Selling Hurdle

runners.name: Madame De Labrunie
runners.spotlight: Won a claiming hurdle in France so at least she knows how to win, and has shown enough in her British races to believe she could easily take care of these now into a seller.
runners.comment: Showed enough in her British races to believe she could easily take care of these

runners.name: Sharp Tor
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a Taunton bumper and his third in a 2m5f maiden here amounts to nothing as only three ran and he was always behind.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a Taunton bumper and his third in a 2m5f maiden here amounts to nothing

runners.name: Stavanger Rose
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£1,200 after pulling up in an Irish point; pulled up in a Newbury novice and then fell mid-race at Chepstow; soon downgraded.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a Newbury novice and then fell mid-race at Chepstow; soon downgraded

runners.name: Upthereforthinking
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden and soft ground wasn't an obvious excuse when tailed off in a 2m4f seller at Leicester in December; now visored.
runners.comment: Soft ground wasn't an obvious excuse when tailed off in a 2m4f seller at Leicester

runners.name: Knight Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Gained a third win on the AW in February (1m6f) but regressed in his last four starts; 0-6 as a hurdler and returns to this discipline for the first time since pulled up in a handicap last summer for Chris Grant; hardly solid but this is a poor race.
runners.comment: 0-6 as a jumper and hardly solid, though did win on the AW in February

runners.name: American Trio
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in points and completely tailed off at huge odds in two maiden hurdles; in a good yard and the visor is new (replacing cheekpieces), but couldn't be confident.
runners.comment: 0-8 in points and completely tailed off at huge odds in two maiden hurdles

runners.name: Getone
runners.spotlight: It wasn't a desperate run when beaten 24l behind a useful horse in a Tramore maiden hurdle in October (yielding); new trainer fits new headgear and lowers this 7yo's sights.
runners.comment: Some promise in Ireland; new trainer fits new headgear and lowers this 7yo's sights

runners.name: Jet Joe
runners.spotlight: Made his debut in a race won by Sober Glory; again tailed off at Hereford in November; dropped in grade with a tongue-tie added; would need to see some confidence behind him.
runners.comment: Tailed off so far; dropped in grade with a tongue-tie added; market confidence needed

races.name: Visit Irish Bloodstock Sales With ITM Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Western Cross
runners.spotlight: Second in a bumper, third in a novice and beaten 12l into fourth on handicap debut over C&D a month ago when fitted in a hood; down 3lb and should be involved.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper, third in a novice and beaten 12l into fourth on handicap debut (C&D)

runners.name: Blacksamssenorita
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle here last season on good to firm and beat four rivals on chase debut at Uttoxeter in November (2m, heavy); the wheels have come off but she's back hurdling now and with blinkers given a go; not ruled out.
runners.comment: The wheels have come off but she's back hurdling now and with blinkers given a go

runners.name: Arnie Moon
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who ran away with a weak handicap at Hereford 13 days ago (2m, good); pushed up 8lb for that but he was strong in the betting and probably has more to offer.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who ran away with a weak handicap at Hereford 13 days ago; raised 8lb

runners.name: Son Of Hypnos
runners.spotlight: Flat, hurdle and chase winner in Ireland; however, went off 150-1 on stable debut here three weeks ago (2m5f handicap hurdle) and failed to complete; the most exposed of these.
runners.comment: 150-1 on stable debut here three weeks ago (2m5f handicap hurdle) and failed to complete

runners.name: Circle Of Secrets
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 15 in a 3yo hurdle in Ireland (good to yielding) when with Emmet Mullins; tailed off on yard debut in a Taunton maiden but the ground was heavy and he was short in the betting at 4-1; interesting handicap debutant with his age allowance.
runners.comment: Unexposed and an interesting handicap debutant with his age allowance

races.name: Ludo Sports & Live Lounge "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bannerdown
runners.spotlight: By Jack Hobbs and out of a stayer so shaped with promise to be a fairly close fourth around the tight turns of Stratford (soft); this stiffer track should work in his favour.
runners.comment: Stoutly bred so shaped well when fourth around the tight turns of Stratford

runners.name: Horethorne
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam well beaten in bumpers but a half-sister to useful winners Wild West Wind (2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase), Whitehotchlilifili (bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle) and Redhotfillypeppers (bumper and 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase); kicks off in a modest race.
runners.comment: Dam well related and this 4yo is kicking off in a modest race

runners.name: Triple Edge
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 12l at Wincanton (good to soft) where he was held up and made some late gains; should improve.
runners.comment: Beaten about 12l at Wincanton (good to soft) where he was held up and made some late gains

runners.name: Jukella
runners.spotlight: Outpaced once the tempo quickened and was beaten in the region of 20l at Huntingdon (good; 18-1).
runners.comment: Outpaced once the tempo quickened and was beaten in the region of 20l at Huntingdon

runners.name: Mysteriosa
runners.spotlight: Sean Bowen was aboard for her debut but she still went off 22-1 at Wincanton (good to soft) and finished tailed off; has since had wind surgery and a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: 22-1 when tailed off at Wincanton; since had wind surgery and a tongue-tie is added

runners.name: Tuscaloosa
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and trailed home a distant last on good ground at Kempton last month.
runners.comment: 100-1 and trailed home a distant last on good ground at Kempton last month

races.name: It Pays To Buy Irish Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Carrigmoorna Rowan
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing for Harry Fry; competitive form over hurdles and fences for current yard but bled two runs back and then ran no race at Bangor.
runners.comment: Has competitive form but bled two runs back and then ran no race at Bangor

runners.name: Prairie Queen
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden but was close up in her final handicap hurdle and ran okay in her first chase (2m2f, good), for all that she was no match for the clearcut winner; should have a role to play.
runners.comment: Ran okay in her first chase (2m2f), for all that she was no match for the clearcut winner

runners.name: Lokis Mischief
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles, including off this mark in just his second handicap (2m7f, good); ran poorly last time but a hood is added for this chase debut and the drop back in trip won't hurt him.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles, including off this mark; hood applied for this chase debut

runners.name: Land Girls Luck
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-16 over hurdles and placed in both her races following a lengthy break; she's not the biggest to be jumping fences but she measured her hurdles well; respected.
runners.comment: Not the biggest to be jumping fences but she measured her hurdles well; respected

runners.name: Majestic Moment
runners.spotlight: Second run over fences when coming home alone after final-fence mayhem at Fontwell in October (2m2f, good to soft); off an untouched mark when a well-held fourth at Newcastle the following month; off since; dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: Lucky to win at Fontwell and then well held off an untouched mark at Newcastle

runners.name: The Pit Popsy
runners.spotlight: Brief hurdling career wasn't devoid of promise but her debut over fences at Hereford in November (2m, good; 28-1) was disappointing, with that also her handicap debut; best watched back from a break.
runners.comment: Hurdle runs not devoid of promise but her chase debut in November was disappointing

runners.name: Holy Legend
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler who hasn't looked a natural over fences, refusing two runs back; opposable.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdler who hasn't looked a natural over fences, refusing two runs back; opposable

runners.name: Hellstopatthetop
runners.spotlight: 0-4 in points and 0-14 under rules (0-9 chasing); out of form late last year and out of the handicap this afternoon.
runners.comment: Maiden; out of form late last year and out of the handicap this afternoon

runners.name: Senor Diaz
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on his chasing debut in a C&D handicap in December; went off 40-1 that day so hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his chasing debut in a C&D handicap in December; went off 40-1 that day

runners.name: Poets Reflection
runners.spotlight: 1-34 overall and 0-10 as a chaser; has been in poor form for a while (hurdling most recently) and couldn't entertain from 11lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Has been in poor form for a while and couldn't entertain from 11lb out of the weights

races.name: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Saint Cyr De Pail
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner, over 2m and 2m3f; last to finish when third over 2m3f here 21 days ago and this represents a significant hike in trip.
runners.comment: Hurdle/chase winner but finished last here 21 days ago; up significantly in trip

runners.name: Dyno Dave
runners.spotlight: Didn't hang about over hurdles and seemed to appreciate getting away from slow ground when edging home in a C&D good-ground handicap three weeks ago, on only his fourth run over fences; up 5lb but probably improving.
runners.comment: Appreciated getting away from slow ground when a narrow winner here three weeks ago

runners.name: Mahler Moon
runners.spotlight: Won 3m handicap hurdle at Newbury last March on good to soft; took seven goes to win over fences but dug deepest last time at Huntingdon (2m7f, good); just 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Dug deepest last time at Huntingdon (2m7f, good); just 2lb higher

races.name: Totnes And Bridgetown Races Company Ltd Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Macklin
runners.spotlight: 7-11 in points and his rules form is useful, having finished third at Cheltenham last May and winning as he liked here last month; looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: 7-11 in points and his rules form is useful, winning here last month; the one to beat

runners.name: Franco De Port
runners.spotlight: Former Grade 1 winner for Willie Mullins but a long time ago; however, after a mammoth absence of 978 days he ran surprisingly well at Warwick (3m, good to soft), finishing under 9l behind Golden Son who went on to finish fourth at Cheltenham last month; this 11yo clearly retains some of his old ability.
runners.comment: Former Grade 1 winner; long absence prior to a very pleasing third at Warwick

runners.name: Stumps Or Slips
runners.spotlight: Bought out of Olly Murphy's yard for Â£26,000; on the retreat but had run well when falling three out at Taunton in January and was close up behind respected opposition in his latest point; could go well.
runners.comment: Fell three out at Taunton in January when running well; decent latest run in a point

runners.name: High Game Royal
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in 2023 for the Hobbs/White operation; beaten in two points before finishing well held at Stratford recently.
runners.comment: Beaten in two points before finishing well held at Stratford

runners.name: Gateau De Miel
runners.spotlight: 18-1 when pulled up in Macklin's race here last month and only third of four runners in subsequent point.
runners.comment: 18-1 when pulled up in Macklin's race here last month

runners.name: Windance
runners.spotlight: Four wins under rules divided between hurdles and fences but at an ordinary level; 66-1 when tailed off behind Macklin here last month.
runners.comment: 66-1 when tailed off behind Macklin here last month

runners.name: Daisy Yeats
runners.spotlight: Sole point win came when finishing alone and that was in 2023; absent since last May.
runners.comment: Sole point win came when finishing alone and that was in 2023; absent since last May

runners.name: Assassins Lullaby
runners.spotlight: Only 2-34 in points and has struggled badly in that scene this year; no appeal.
runners.comment: Only 2-34 in points and has struggled badly in that scene this year; no appeal

races.name: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Galileo Charm
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who gained a deserved breakthrough success in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 22 days ago; up 3lb but she's not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win at Wolverhampton 22 days ago; up 3lb but she's not taken lightly

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7f Wolverhampton novice success and she has performed with credit since, challenging wide when seventh in 7f Lingfield handicap 11 days ago; she's no forlorn hope off an easing mark.
runners.comment: Challenged wide when solid Lingfield seventh latest; no forlorn hope off easing mark

runners.name: My Shagaf
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden who went too freely tried in cheekpieces when last of ten in 7f Lingfield handicap 11 days ago; could bounce back now the headgear is dispensed with.
runners.comment: Too free in cheekpieces at Lingfield latest; could bounce back now headgear is removed

runners.name: Spirit Of Jenny
runners.spotlight: Proved reliable without getting her head in front in 2025, signing off a respectable eighth in 6f handicap here in September; not out of things on her return.
runners.comment: Proved reliable without getting her head in front in 2025; not out of things on return

runners.name: Pickersgill
runners.spotlight: Fair ex-Irish 6f winner who got right back on track for her new yard under more patient tactics when second of nine in 7f Wolverhampton handicap three days ago; a player nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Back on track for new yard with Wolverhampton second three days ago; player nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Kempton (7f) in October but she came in last of 12 in 1m Lingfield handicap in December; needs to bounce back after a break.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Kempton in October but came in last of 12 in Lingfield h'cap in December

runners.name: Maybe Soon
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Supremacy who has been brought along steadily, not enjoying a clear run when ninth in 7f Newcastle maiden 19 days ago; she can take a step forward now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Brought along steadily; she can take a step forward now going handicapping

runners.name: Khuskhas
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Yarmouth maiden last June but she failed to kick on in nurseries after, beating just one at Lingfield (7f) in October on her final start for Kevin Philippart De Foy; changed hands for 11,000gns subsequently and she goes in blinkers for her new yard.
runners.comment: Scored at Yarmouth but failed to kick on in nurseries after; blinkers on for new yard

runners.name: Seapearl
runners.spotlight: Never a threat in a trio of 7f maidens, not knocked about when sixth at Lingfield in February; this Calyx filly looks to have more to offer on her first go in handicap company after a break.
runners.comment: Never a threat in a trio of 7f maidens; more to offer on her first go in handicap company

races.name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Brazilian Belle
runners.spotlight: In fine form since switched to the AW this winter, going in over C&D in February before following up in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 18 days ago; up another 2lb but not taken lightly in her hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: C&D winner and followed up at Wolverhampton latest; not taken lightly in hat-trick bid

runners.name: Blue Lakota
runners.spotlight: Course scorer who arrives in good nick, just edged out when fourth of six in 5f Newcastle handicap 19 days ago; can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Course scorer who arrives in good nick; can make his presence felt again

runners.name: Binadham
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but he comes here on the back of a good third of nine to Brazilian Belle in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 18 days ago; well in the mix off the same mark with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but good third at Wolverhampton 18 days ago; well in the mix

runners.name: Toomuchforme
runners.spotlight: Opened her account for her current yard over C&D in March but bumped start when last of eight in 5f Wolverhampton handicap three days ago; it would come as no surprise to see her bounce back.
runners.comment: C&D winner; bumped start when last at Wolverhampton three days ago; can bounce back here

runners.name: Castan
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who wasn't seen to ideal effect when eighth of 12 in 5f Newcastle handicap last month, helping set a strong gallop; not out of things eased 1lb.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; not seen to ideal effect when eighth at Newcastle latest; not out of things

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways in 5f Beverley handicap but caught wide when a solid seventh there later in August; needs to hit the ground running on his Tapeta debut after seven months off the track.
runners.comment: Caught wide when Beverley seventh in August; needs to hit ground running on Tapeta debut
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Smooth Silesie
runners.spotlight: Likeable front runner who signed off for 2025 with a fourth 5f victory at Yarmouth in October; shaped as if in need of the run after five months off when last of six at Wolverhampton (5f) two weeks ago and she can take a sizeable step forward here with usual blinkers also refitted.
runners.comment: Last at Wolverhampton two weeks ago but can take big step forward with blinkers refitted

runners.name: Dark Kestrel
runners.spotlight: Already a two-time 5f scorer at Newcastle in 2026 and not discredited when sixth of nine to Brazilian Belle in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 18 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: Solid sixth of nine in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 18 days ago; considered again

runners.name: Fierce
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f scorer in 2025 but he's been off since coming in last of seven in 6f seller here in October; handily weighted if resurgent after a break though.
runners.comment: Off since last in seller here in October; handily weighted if resurgent after a break

races.name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Magical Merlot
runners.spotlight: Ended four-race 2yo campaign with clear win in Brighton novice (1m, good to firm) in October, having previously run well over 7f on Tapeta here; gelded during 173-day break; open to improvement in handicaps this year.
runners.comment: Ended four-race 2yo campaign with clearcut novice win; open to improvement this year

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who came from the rear for respectable fifth on recent seasonal/handicap debut here (7f); that race probably wasn't run to suit him, and the dam's side of his pedigree strongly suggests this longer trip can show him in a better light.
runners.comment: Kept on from the rear for fifth on recent seasonal/handicap debut (7f); can improve

runners.name: Island Bear
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise on all three 2yo starts, most recently when sixth in valuable maiden at Glorious Goodwood (7f, good to soft); makes handicap/AW debut after eight-month absence; cheekpieces removed after one use; market helpful.
runners.comment: Showed clear promise on all three 2yo starts; makes handicap debut after 248-day break

runners.name: Captain Fox
runners.spotlight: Delivered on 2yo promise when dominating five-runner Lingfield novice (1m) from the front in February; unlikely to have things all his own way in this much stronger race but probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Dominated Lingfield novice from the front in February; moves into handicaps with potential

runners.name: Gatehouse
runners.spotlight: Built upon promising debut (1m) when beating good yardstick to score at Chelmsford (1m2f) in December; not seen since and this drop back in trip is not an obvious move but, nevertheless, he enters calculations.
runners.comment: Built upon promising debut when winning 1m2f novice in December; back in trip here

runners.name: Deported
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 7f AW maidens on the autumn; beaten at 8-11 odds when third on latter occasion but that form looks better now than it did at the time; gelded during the winter; ought to be suited by the step up in trip on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed in two 7f AW maidens in the autumn; ought to be suited by this longer trip

runners.name: Sovereign View
runners.spotlight: Came good at fifth attempt, winning five-runner Newcastle nursery (1m) from off the pace in October; not seen since but remains unexposed over this trip; contender if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Absent since breakthrough win at Newcastle in October but still unexposed as a miler

races.name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Faithful Dream
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he arrives in very good nick, not seen to ideal effect when third of nine in 6f Chelmsford handicap 12 days ago; very much one to consider eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Remains winless but he arrives in very good nick; very much one to consider eased 1lb

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of 7f Wolverhampton novices for Clive Cox on his first two runs but he's failed to beat a rival in two outings for his new yard, last of seven over C&D 12 days ago; could bounce back if becoming more tractable though.
runners.comment: Last of seven over C&D 12 days ago; could bounce back if becoming more tractable though

runners.name: Eightthreeone
runners.spotlight: Rumble Inthejungle gelding who showed fair form last year but wasn't seen out after posting a good third in 5f Lingfield maiden ten months ago; needs to hit the ground running on his return.
runners.comment: Off since a good third at Lingfield ten months ago; needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Reliable filly who comes here on the back of a solid third of four in 6f Kempton novice 20 days ago; she may do better still now stepping into handicaps.
runners.comment: Solid third at Kempton 20 days ago; she may do better still now stepping into handicaps

runners.name: Real Jack
runners.spotlight: Much improved on his final run at two when a running-on second of nine in 5f Newcastle novice in November; this son of Ubettabelieveit appeals as a likely improver now going handicapping back up in trip; player.
runners.comment: Much-improved Newcastle second in November; improver now h'capping back up in trip; player

runners.name: Fanjove
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of 7f nurseries at two and also ended 2025 with a good second of three in 6f Newcastle nursery; has since been gelded and can't be discounted on his return.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner at two; has since been gelded and can't be discounted on his return
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

runners.name: Go Victor
runners.spotlight: Scored at Thirsk (7f) last June but he ended 2025 well out of sorts; he needs to take a major step forward on his first go on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Scored at Thirsk last June but ended 2025 well out of sorts; needs a major step forward

runners.name: Magic Lady Mae
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly who has hinted at promise, fading late when sixth of nine in 6f Lingfield novice 13 days ago; she can take a step forward for her in-form handler on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Mehmas filly who has hinted at promise; can take a step forward on her handicap debut

runners.name: Paranjape
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Wolverhampton (6f) in November and backed it up with a solid fifth on his nursery debut at Chelmsford (7f) following month, caught wide; no forlorn hope after a break.
runners.comment: Solid fifth at Chelmsford in December, caught wide; no forlorn hope after a break
runners.owner: G H Thompson & S P C Woods

runners.name: Sagremor
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when fourth on his nursery debut at Doncaster in November but this keen-going son of Armor could still have more to offer on his return having been gelded.
runners.comment: Fourth at Doncaster final run; could still have more to offer on return having been gelded

runners.name: Explosive Finnish
runners.spotlight: Son of Pinatubo who signed off for 2025 with a good third of nine in 5f novice here in November; since gelded and he's one to consider now stepping into handicap company.
runners.comment: Good third here in November; since gelded and considered stepping into handicap company

runners.name: Sprinting Speed
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily in a trio of runs for Richard Hannon last summer, not knocked about when eighth in 6f Newmarket novice in August; changed hands for 8,000gns subsequently and this son of Mehmas is not out of things on his yard/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Eighth at Newmarket in August; not out of things on his yard/handicap debut here

races.name: Sky Sports Racing Mile Series Final Handicap (CCR Mile Series Final)

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Finished behind four of these at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last month, but had been off since breaking his duck at Nottingham (1m, good) in October; worth a second look with the run under his belt on his first visit here, especially if backed.
runners.comment: May have needed last month's reappearance; worth a second look, especially if backed

runners.name: Signcastle City
runners.spotlight: 4-23 on turf and 1-13 on the AW; behind three of these at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last month and well held in one visit here; stable also runs Gennadius.
runners.comment: 1-13 on AW and behind three of these at Chelmsford last month; well held in one visit here

runners.name: Studious
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on Tapeta at Newcastle and Wolverhampton and was placed in both starts over this C&D in the autumn; that makes this consistent sort a player.
runners.comment: Consistent sort who was placed in both starts over this C&D in the autumn; a player

runners.name: Tonal
runners.spotlight: Ultra-consistent in AW handicaps (34144271121131); raised 6lb for last month's Chelmsford success (1m, Polytrack), but is the only previous course winner in the field which he did over this trip in August; one of five runners for the yard; much respected.
runners.comment: Ultra-consistent on AW; (34144271121131); only course winner in the field; much respected

runners.name: Gennadius
runners.spotlight: Often placed, but just 1-18 and well held on his first visit here four weeks ago; has it all to do from the outside stall.
runners.comment: 1-18 and well held on first visit here four weeks ago; all to do from the outside stall

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at Chelmsford (7f, Polytrack) this year; has won over this far, but well held in one start over C&D a year ago; others stronger.
runners.comment: Has won over this far, but well held in one start over C&D a year ago

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Having only his second start for the yard when beating 21 rivals in an amateur riders' handicap at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) nine days ago; has run well here before and shortlisted despite his 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Beat 21 rivals at Doncaster nine days ago; shortlisted despite his 5lb penalty

runners.name: Lexington Jet
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) this year and he had five of these behind him there last time; would have been a major player if this race had been run at that venue despite another 5lb rise, but one visit here proved inconclusive as regards the suitability of this track.
runners.comment: In fine form at Chelmsford this year, but the suitability of this track remains unclear

runners.name: Uniting
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this trip at Kempton (Polytrack) and Newcastle (Tapeta) in November; fair third of seven behind two of these at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last month; each-way claims down another 1lb if taking to this track at the first time of asking.
runners.comment: Third behind two of these at Chelmsford last month; each-way claims down another 1lb

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: Less than 2l behind Lexington Jet when second of seven at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last month and 4lb better off; yet to win in ten visits here, but has made the frame eight times; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Yet to win in ten visits here, but has made the frame eight times; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Law Of Design
runners.spotlight: Some fair turf form for Brian Meehan, but despite plummeting in the weights has shown little in three AW starts for this yard, failing to beat a rival over 7f here in January; cheekpieces given a go, but some way down the stable's pecking order.
runners.comment: Shown little in three AW starts for this yard; some way down the stable's pecking order

runners.name: Echalar
runners.spotlight: Back off the same mark as when winning at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) for Jane Chapple-Hyam in January, but quiet in three starts since, including two for this yard; stable looks to have stronger candidates elsewhere.
runners.comment: Quiet in three starts since winning of this mark at Wolverhampton in January

runners.name: Giant
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than for his latest win at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last August, but hasn't been firing of late; first visit here.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark, but hasn't been firing of late; first visit here

races.name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Trojan Soldier
runners.spotlight: 0-9, but runner-up in four of his five handicaps including narrow defeats over 1m6f/16.5f here in both starts since returning in January; a player again with cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: 0-9, but narrowly beaten in his last two starts here and a player again; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Atheneum
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who finished runner-up in two of his six starts on turf in Ireland last summer (both over this trip), but failed to beat a rival on stable debut/reappearance at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) last month; something to prove.
runners.comment: 0-7; failed to beat a rival on stable debut at Wolverhampton last month; enough to prove

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f wins on turf last year and was returning from eight months off when beaten a short head at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack) 13 days ago; 2lb higher, but the return to a longer trip should suit; respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head on his reappearance 13 days ago; return to a longer trip should suit

runners.name: Clansman
runners.spotlight: Has done well in three starts at Newcastle since returning from seven months off in January, making all over 1m4f last month; stays at least this far and still feasibly treated despite a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Made all over 1m4f at Newcastle last month; stays this far; still feasibly treated
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

races.name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gesayed
runners.spotlight: Created pretty good impression when keeping on too strongly for odds-on favourite at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) last June, shaping as though this extra furlong would suit; not seen since but remains a good prospect.
runners.comment: Created good impression when winning over 6f on debut at Hamilton last June

runners.name: Merry
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a Group-placed 5f 2yo winner; outran 40-1 odds when creditable third of 11 on debut at Kempton (7f) in December, and that performance suggests she'll play a big role here.
runners.comment: Creditable third of 11 on debut at Kempton (7f) in December and a likely contender
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing And R Mitchell

runners.name: Confide In Me
runners.spotlight: 2,500gns 2yo; fourth foal; half-sister to an Italian 1m2f winner; dam unraced half-sister to winners Red Box (1m Listed) and Untold Secret (useful 1m-1m2f), out of French Oaks winner Confidential Lady; has fairly good standard to reach on debut.
runners.comment: Out of a well-connected mare but has fairly good standard to reach on debut

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: First foal of a once-raced, well-related mare; struggled when 40-1 for debut over C&D ten days ago.
runners.comment: Struggled when 40-1 for debut over C&D ten days ago

runners.name: Gilleangirl
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging debut when fifth of nine at Newcastle (7f) in October but finished last over C&D in February and remains best watched.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging debut in October but finished last over C&D in February

runners.name: Korbut
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to 7f Group 1 winner Good Guess and 7f Group 3 winner Spangled; towards rear throughout her debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f) a fortnight ago and needs to be a completely different proposition here.
runners.comment: Has a good pedigree but was towards rear throughout her recent debut

runners.name: Porth Eilian
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' Listed winner Point Lynas; made a low-key debut in the autumn (6f, turf) but ought to be capable of better at some stage this year.
runners.comment: Made a low-key debut in the autumn but ought to be capable of better this year
runners.owner: Julie & David Martin Dan Hall & Partner

runners.name: Bear Steps
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten when seventh of eight on both starts in the autumn, the latter over C&D.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when seventh of eight on both starts in the autumn, the latter over C&D

races.name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Cuban Girl
runners.spotlight: Brighton winner for Andrew Balding (1m, good to firm) last summer, but well beaten in both starts for this yard and has shown little in three goes on the AW; plenty to prove after six months off.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both starts for this yard and little in three goes on AW; off six months

runners.name: Wrydcroft
runners.spotlight: Won two Leicester handicaps (7f, good to soft) in the autumn, but got stuck in the mud at Nottingham (1m, heavy) on his final outing; makes his Tapeta debut after 166 days off; watch market.
runners.comment: Dual Leicester handicap winner in the autumn; makes his Tapeta debut after 166 days off
runners.owner: G Libson And Mrs P Sly

runners.name: Raft Up
runners.spotlight: Placed behind the prolific Yorkshire Glory in his last two starts at Newcastle, but has finished out of the frame in all six visits here and attempts this far for the first time on his 35th outing.
runners.comment: Placed in last two starts, but modest form here and attempts this far for the first time

runners.name: Velvet Skies
runners.spotlight: 3-47 with his wins coming over 6f/7f on turf/Polytrack in Ireland; has made a low-key start for this yard and needs to improve for the fitting of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 3-47; has made a low-key start for this yard; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Camera Shy
runners.spotlight: Not at his best at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) 11 days ago, but had previously narrowly beaten the winner of that race over this C&D; drawn wide, but still respected if bouncing back now returned here.
runners.comment: Not at his best at Newcastle last time, but had previously won over this C&D; respected
runners.owner: The Horse Watchers 13 And Michael Dods

runners.name: Reginald Charles
runners.spotlight: Record on the AW last year read 1161 including two wins over C&D; not at his best when behind two of these back here in February, but more than capable of making an impact if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 2-4 over C&D; not at his best last time, but capable of making an impact if bouncing back

runners.name: Bomb Squad
runners.spotlight: Eight wins on the AW including three over 6f/7f here; still 2lb above his highest winning mark and will need things to pan out right from the outside stall, but one for the shortlist nonetheless.
runners.comment: Three wins over 6f/7f here; drawn widest but still one for the shortlist

runners.name: King David
runners.spotlight: Often there or thereabouts, but just 1-22 despite sliding down the weights; drawn wide and others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Just 1-22 despite sliding down the weights; others appeal more for win purposes

runners.name: Federal Envoy
runners.spotlight: All three wins over C&D, the latest off 11lb higher in February last year; recent efforts don't suggest he is about to take advantage, though.
runners.comment: Three wins over C&D, the latest off 11lb higher; not suggesting he can take advantage
runners.owner: Lets Be Lucky Racing 37 & David Carroll

runners.name: Embarked
runners.spotlight: Front-runner and C&D winner, but patchy form since his latest success last summer and was well held in a 0-50 classified event here four week ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Front-runner and C&D winner, but patchy form since his latest success; others preferred

runners.name: Fools Rush In
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than when gaining his second C&D success in February, but that is a result of four quiet runs since; not quite sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, the latest off 2lb lower; quiet in last four runs, though
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Moby Quick
runners.spotlight: 1-19 and out of the frame in his last seven starts, including six for this yard; they have mainly been over further, though, and he is now 3lb below his winning mark; could go well at a price with a visor fitted.
runners.comment: 3lb below his sole winning mark; could go well at a price with a visor fitted

runners.name: Saisons dOr
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over C&D and 4lb below his last winning mark, but now at the veteran stage and has beaten a total of one rival in three starts since returning from five months off in February.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, but has beaten just one rival in three starts since returning in February

races.name: Prix Djebel (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)

runners.name: Crown Relic
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten British raider who debuted in February and has only raced on the AW so far, both times over 7f at Wolverhampton, making a significant jump forward on his second run under Shane Foley; bred to be above-average and holds a 2000 Guineas entry plus one in the Poulains; interesting.
runners.comment: Run twice and won twice, last time easily; interesting prospect

runners.name: Samangan
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile who maintained his upwards curve throughout, ending with a 2.5l success in the 6f Group 2 Criterium de Maisons-Laffitte at Chantilly in October on soft; won a 6f Group 3 at the same track prior to that, meaning there is little doubting his ability, so just needs to prove he can see out 7f (his Lope De Vega half-sister won over a mile) , which his pedigree suggests he might; entered in the 2000 Guineas, Poulains and Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo who holds lots of big race entries; strong chance

runners.name: Afandy
runners.spotlight: Progressive gelding, who heads into this off a career-best effort, which came over 7.5f at Cagnes-Sur-Mer in a Listed event on very soft behind a filly who lines up for the Prix Imprudence later on this card; has race-fitness on his side, so it'll be no big surprise if he's involved in the finish.
runners.comment: Smart gelding who has race-fitness on his side; can play a part

runners.name: Michael The Great
runners.spotlight: Won two 6.5f contests here on the AW as a 2yo but was then beaten, albeit not far, at Chantilly in a 5.5f Listed event last month; enough in his pedigree suggests he'll get 7f but tough task all the same.
runners.comment: Won two 6.5f contests here on the AW as a 2yo but was then beaten on return; tough task

runners.name: Bourgoin
runners.spotlight: Won a conditions event on the AW track here over 6.5f in December (form looks modest) but then was well beaten in a 6.5f Listed event at Chantilly, again on the AW, about a month ago; clearly capable of better, but needs to prove he goes on turf in what looks a decent contest; entered in the Poulains.
runners.comment: Well beaten in Listed company last time and yet to race on turf

runners.name: Cielo Di Roma
runners.spotlight: Done plenty of racing compared to some, and was in front of Dostoievsky when winning here over 7f on the round course  (good to soft) in August; far from disgraced in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere after that and didn't shape too badly in a mile Listed event at Saint-Cloud last time on heavy; lacks the improvement some may have, but helps sets a level; entered in the Poulains.
runners.comment: Helps set to level to overturn but lacks the scope of some
runners.owner: Mike Pietrangelo Mag Horse Racing Srl et al

runners.name: Romanticon
runners.spotlight: Related to a couple of multiple winners, who were at least very useful types, and won his first two races at 2yo at La Zarzuela in Spain before finishing second there over a mile; impossible to know what to make of that form but can't be completely dismissed on debut for new trainer.
runners.comment: Yet to race in France but knows how to win and isn't completely dismissed

runners.name: Take Me On
runners.spotlight: Created a huge impression when landing a mile event here on the round course by a wide margin (second and fourth have won since) on heavy having carted his way to the front under Mickael Barzalona (who is claimed to ride Samangan), looking every inch a Group race performer in the process; holds a 2000 Guineas entry plus ones in the Poulains and Prix du Jockey Club, and is an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Bolted up here on his debut over a mile on the round course; exciting prospect

runners.name: Dostoievsky
runners.spotlight: Very useful sort, who was beaten on debut here when 2l behind Cielo Di Roma over 7f on the round track, but ended off his 2yo campaign with a Chantilly Listed race success over this trip on soft ground, not needing to match his previous first winning level in doing so; Christophe Soumillon back aboard.
runners.comment: Listed winner when last seen and has Christophe Soumillon back aboard
runners.owner: Alain Jathiere Gousserie Racing & Gerard Augustin-Normand

runners.name: Firefall
runners.spotlight: Impressive in thrashing his rivals by 4.5l on debut over 1m1f 110yds on the AW at Chantilly in November, having been keen in front, and bred to be decent, related to a few winners, notably dual Group 2 winner Threat; could be anything; entered in the Poulains and Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Impressive in thrashing his rivals over 1m1f 110yds on the AW at Chantilly in November

races.name: Prix Imprudence (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Half Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Ran seven times as a 2yo, progressing from two novice wins (both 7f on good to soft) to finishing her season with a Group 3 win at Chantilly over 7f on very soft, when Baklawa was behind; seems to handle all ground so has to be a player if she's trained on; entered in the Pouliches.
runners.comment: Group winner as a 2yo; has to be a player if she's trained on

runners.name: Quiescent
